text: Working with APIs allows developers to connect different software applications. Most APIs use HTTP requests to communicate, such as GET to retrieve data or POST to create new data. The data is often sent and received in JSON format, which is lightweight and easy to read. Many APIs also require authentication, usually through an API key, to ensure only authorised users can access the service. It's important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period. Finally, always include error handling in your code. This helps you manage problems like incorrect requests or server issues gracefully.
